title: lathyrus maritimus f. albescens
intro: Lathyrus maritimus f. albescens, also known as sea pease or white sea pea, is a rare, white-flowering variant of the Lathyrus maritimus plant, a type of wild pea native to the coastal areas of Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus maritimus f. albescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and flowers are used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
